issues.title: Inadequate Error Handling in Ingestion Command
issues.details: The ingestion command does not handle errors robustly, especially during the upsert process. If an error occurs, it should stop processing and provide a clear error message. Suggested fix: Implement try-catch blocks around critical operations and log errors appropriately. File: .claude/commands/ingest.md:3-5.

issues.title: Potential Path Traversal Vulnerability
issues.details: The ingestion command allows arbitrary file paths as input without validation, which could lead to path traversal attacks. Suggested fix: Validate and sanitize input paths to ensure they are within expected directories. File: .claude/commands/ingest.md:1-2.

issues.title: Missing Unit Tests for New Logic
issues.details: The new ingestion logic lacks unit tests to ensure correctness and reliability. Suggested fix: Write unit tests covering various scenarios for the ingestion command. File: .claude/commands/ingest.md.

issues.title: Inconsistent Commit Message Format
issues.details: Commit messages do not consistently follow the specified format, which can lead to confusion in tracking changes. Suggested fix: Enforce a commit message template across the team. File: .claude/commands/features.md.

summary: The overall code quality shows several areas for improvement, particularly in error handling, security, and testing. Addressing these issues will enhance the robustness and security of the ingestion process.
